title: Clinopodium plicatulum
intro: Clinopodium plicatulum, also known as mountain calamintha or plicate clinopodium, is a perennial herb in the mint family (Lamiaceae) native to the mountainous regions of the Mediterranean. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which may help to soothe digestive issues and reduce stress. The bioactive constituents of Clinopodium plicatulum include essential oils such as borneol and bornyl acetate, flavonoids like luteolin and apigenin, and phenolic acids like rosmarinic acid. The leaves and flowers of Clinopodium plicatulum are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as to flavor liqueurs and spirits.
taxonomy: Clinopodium plicatulum is a species of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae, commonly known as the mint family. It is native to North America. The plant is classified as a herbaceous perennial and is a member of the Lamiaceae family. Clinopodium plicatulum is closely related to other species within the Clinopodium genus, which also comprises various species of flowering plants. The exact classification of Clinopodium plicatulum is still subject to further research and study.
